text.title: AI Design Assistant Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the ai-design-assistant-patterns methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Exactly ONE assistant pattern (sidebar / modal / inline / review) MUST be chosen per tool; mixing is forbidden in the same surface.
rule.rationale: Mixed patterns force users to learn two mental models in one tool.

rule.statement: When the trigger is an agent (CI, scheduled job, batch) the pattern MUST be review-mode with structured JSON output.
rule.rationale: Sidebar / inline assume a human at the keyboard; agents have no keyboard.

rule.statement: Sidebar pattern MUST only be chosen when the context window remains valid ≥3 user actions.
rule.rationale: Context-shifting faster than 3 actions makes sidebar suggestions stale.

rule.statement: Modal pattern MUST cap user input at 3 clicks before submission.
Every pattern MUST emit accept / reject / ignore telemetry per interaction.
rule.rationale: Without telemetry pattern fit cannot be revisited.

rule.statement: When preconditions hold, run the full procedure end-to-end and emit the output artefact.
rule.rationale: Routes positive decision-tree leaves to the procedure.

rule.statement: When preconditions fail or skip-conditions hold, exit without producing the artefact and document why.
rule.rationale: Routes negative decision-tree leaves out without polluting the output channel.
